text.title: Speech-to-Text Advanced Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for production advanced transcription (diarisation, word timestamps, vocab bias, streaming).

rule.statement: Speaker diarisation uses the provider's native feature; never stitch labels with heuristics from a non-diarising provider.
rule.rationale: Heuristic diarisation (segment-by-pause, etc.) is unreliable across speakers with similar pace; native diarisation uses voice embeddings.

rule.statement: Word-level timestamps are obtained via the provider's word_timestamps=true parameter; never derive from segment timestamps.
rule.rationale: Segment-level start/end cannot align captions on a per-word cadence.

rule.statement: Domain vocabulary is passed as a provider parameter (keywords / hotwords / vocab boost), not concatenated into the prompt.
rule.rationale: Prompts get truncated and ignored; provider-native vocab is hot-loaded into the decoder.

rule.statement: Sub-300ms streaming uses Deepgram / AssemblyAI / Azure Realtime WebSocket; never stitch overlapping Whisper batch windows.
rule.rationale: Stitching adds 1-5s latency and produces drift at window boundaries.

rule.statement: Speaker hint count is passed to providers that accept it (n_speakers / max_speakers); never let the model guess.
rule.rationale: Without a hint, providers over-segment ("Speaker 1" → "Speaker 5" within a 2-speaker meeting).

rule.statement: Post-process the transcript: punctuation restore (if not native), filler removal, abbreviation expansion. Always run; never ship raw provider output.
rule.rationale: Raw output contains "uh"/"um", missing punctuation, expanded numbers ("two thousand five") — readability suffers.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire advanced TranscriptionService per procedure.
Single-speaker non-streaming with no vocab bias → use speech-to-text-basics.
